title: melanthiaceae
intro: The melanthiaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the mayflower or wake-robin family. This family is characterized by its unique, often greenish or yellowish flowers that grow in clusters, and includes a diverse range of species with varying growth habits and leaf forms. The melanthiaceae family is found in temperate regions of the world, including North America, Europe, and Asia, and comprises around 40-50 genera with over 400 species. Many species within this family are valued for their ornamental value and are cultivated in gardens for their attractive flowers and foliage.
